metadata.convention: Flowering_Time = days from planting to flowering. decreased = earlier flowering (fewer days). increased = later flowering (more days). Baseline = WT unless noted.

evidence.title: Sorghum Phytochrome B Inhibits Flowering in Long Days
evidence.authors: Yang S, Weers BD, Morishige DT, Mullet JE
evidence.evidence_sentence: Loss of PhyB (58M phyB-1 vs 100M PHYB)...Long Days (14h) 62 vs 126 days...EARLY (~60 days earlier).
evidence.claim: ma3^R phyB null flowers ~60 days earlier than Ma3 WT under LD

evidence.title: Sorghum Phytochrome B Inhibits Flowering in Long Days
evidence.authors: Yang S, Weers BD, Morishige DT, Mullet JE
evidence.evidence_sentence: Loss of PhyB...Short Days (10h) 48 vs 59 days...EARLY (~11 days earlier).
evidence.claim: ma3^R flowers ~11 days earlier than Ma3 WT under SD

evidence.title: Coincident light and clock regulation of PRR37 controls photoperiodic flowering in sorghum
evidence.authors: Murphy RL, Klein RR, Morishige DT, Brady JA, Rooney WL, Miller FR, Dugas DV, Klein PE, Mullet JE
evidence.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
evidence.evidence_sentence: SM100 Sbprr37-1 (frameshift)...Early flowering...Photoperiod-insensitive.
evidence.claim: Sbprr37-1 frameshift mutant flowers early in LD

evidence.title: Coincident light and clock regulation of PRR37 controls photoperiodic flowering in sorghum
evidence.authors: Murphy RL, Klein RR, Morishige DT, Brady JA, Rooney WL, Miller FR, Dugas DV, Klein PE, Mullet JE
evidence.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
evidence.evidence_sentence: BTx406 Sbprr37-1 (frameshift)...Early flowering.
evidence.claim: BTx406 sbprr37-1 allele flowers early in LD

evidence.title: Coincident light and clock regulation of PRR37 controls photoperiodic flowering in sorghum
evidence.authors: Murphy RL, Klein RR, Morishige DT, Brady JA, Rooney WL, Miller FR, Dugas DV, Klein PE, Mullet JE
evidence.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
evidence.evidence_sentence: Blackhull Kafir Sbprr37-2 (Lys162Asn)...Early flowering.
evidence.claim: SbPRR37 K162N missense mutation confers early flowering

evidence.title: Coincident light and clock regulation of PRR37 controls photoperiodic flowering in sorghum
evidence.authors: Murphy RL, Klein RR, Morishige DT, Brady JA, Rooney WL, Miller FR, Dugas DV, Klein PE, Mullet JE
evidence.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
evidence.evidence_sentence: ATx623 Sbprr37-3 (dual mutations)...Early flowering...Lys162Asn + Q270Stop.
evidence.claim: ATx623 double SbPRR37 mutation flowers early

evidence.title: Ghd7 (Ma6) Represses Sorghum Flowering in Long Days
evidence.authors: Murphy RL, Morishige DT, Brady JA, Rooney WL, Yang S, Klein PE, Mullet JE
evidence.evidence_sentence: The sequence of ghd7-1 from Tx623 (ma6) contained a five base insertion, creating a frameshift and premature termination of the protein...ma6 conferred earlier flowering in LDs.
evidence.claim: ghd7-1 frameshift allele confers early LD flowering

evidence.title: Sorghum SbGhd7 is a major regulator of floral transition
evidence.authors: Tadesse T, Lugassi N, Dragonas B, Shmuel YH, Gladman N, Yuan Y, Weers B, Mullet JE, Pasternak T, Leshkowitz D, Levy AA
evidence.evidence_sentence: SbGhd7 overexpression (OE) completely prevented sorghum flowering.
evidence.claim: SbGhd7 OE completely prevents flowering

evidence.title: Sorghum Phytochrome B Inhibits Flowering in Long Days
evidence.authors: Yang S, Weers BD, Morishige DT, Mullet JE
evidence.evidence_sentence: Loss of Ma5 (ma5) vs Ma5 Long Days...Reduced flowering delay...EARLY.
evidence.claim: ma5 phyC loss confers earlier LD flowering

evidence.title: Photoperiod response and floral transition in sorghum
evidence.authors: Cai Y, Li Q, Liu X, Liu G, Wang L, Li Q, Ma T, Zhang Z
evidence.evidence_sentence: Ma5 is a photoperiod-dependent flowering repressor (LD-specific).
evidence.claim: ma5 shows little effect under SD (SD already permissive)

evidence.title: Maturity2, a novel regulator of flowering time in Sorghum bicolor
evidence.authors: Casto AL, McKinley BA, Yu KMJ, Rooney WL, Mullet JE
evidence.evidence_sentence: 80M (ma2) flowers earlier than 100M (Ma2)...ma1ma2 genotypes flower earlier than ma1Ma2.
evidence.claim: ma2 L141* mutant flowers ~100 days earlier than Ma2 under LD

evidence.title: Sorghum SbGhd7 is a major regulator of floral transition
evidence.authors: Tadesse T, Lugassi N, Dragonas B, Shmuel YH, Gladman N, Yuan Y, Weers B, Mullet JE, Pasternak T, Leshkowitz D, Levy AA
evidence.evidence_sentence: SbEhd1OE activated the promoters of three functional florigens (SbFT1, SbFT8 and SbFT10), dramatically accelerating flowering.
evidence.claim: SbEhd1 OE dramatically accelerates flowering

evidence.title: An Agrobacterium-delivered CRISPR/Cas9 system for targeted mutagenesis in sorghum
evidence.authors: Char SN, Wei J, Mu Q, Li X, Zhang ZJ, Yu J, Yang B
evidence.evidence_sentence: plants containing knockout mutations flowered 8 days later than wild-type plants on average, and the flowering time difference for the second planting date between edited plants and wild types was 10 days.
evidence.claim: SbFT (Sobic.010G045100) CRISPR KO delays flowering by 8-10 days

evidence.title: A sorghum gigantea mutant attenuates florigen gene expression and delays flowering time
evidence.authors: Abdul-Awal SM, Chen J, Xin Z, Harmon FG
evidence.evidence_sentence: sbgi-ems1 homozygous plants averaged 30 days more than sbgi-ems1 heterozygous and SbGI WT siblings.
evidence.claim: sbgi-ems1 EMS nonsense delays LD flowering by 30 days

evidence.title: A sorghum gigantea mutant attenuates florigen gene expression and delays flowering time
evidence.authors: Abdul-Awal SM, Chen J, Xin Z, Harmon FG
evidence.evidence_sentence: DTB and DTA both were increased in sbgi-ems1 plants by an average of 35 days.
evidence.claim: sbgi-ems1 delays SD flowering by 35 days

evidence.title: Sorghum bicolor INDETERMINATE1 is a conserved primary regulator of flowering
evidence.authors: Gladman N, Goodwin S, Chougule K, Richard McCombie W, Ware D
evidence.evidence_sentence: sbid1-1 (P155S, weak): 35-day delay (field); 6-day delay (greenhouse).
evidence.claim: sbid1-1 weak allele delays flowering 35 days in field

evidence.title: Sorghum bicolor INDETERMINATE1 is a conserved primary regulator of flowering
evidence.authors: Gladman N, Goodwin S, Chougule K, Richard McCombie W, Ware D
evidence.evidence_sentence: sbid1-2 (Q199*, strong nonsense): ~170-day delay; near-complete flowering block.
evidence.claim: sbid1-2 strong nonsense delays flowering ~170 days

evidence.title: Sorghum bicolor INDETERMINATE1 is a conserved primary regulator of flowering
evidence.authors: Gladman N, Goodwin S, Chougule K, Richard McCombie W, Ware D
evidence.evidence_sentence: sbid1-1/sbid1-2 compound: Phenotype matches strong sbid1-2; confirms loss-of-function.
evidence.claim: sbid1-1/sbid1-2 compound mutant delays flowering ~170 days

evidence.title: CONSTANS is a photoperiod regulated activator of flowering in sorghum
evidence.authors: Yang S, Murphy RL, Morishige DT, Klein PE, Rooney WL, Mullet JE
evidence.evidence_sentence: SbCO...functions as an activator of flowering in long and short days in sorghum genotypes using RILs with null versions of Sbprr37-1 and Sbghd7-1...Sbco-3 null.
evidence.claim: SbCO null delays flowering when PRR37/Ghd7 are also null

evidence.title: Photoperiod response and floral transition in sorghum
evidence.authors: Cai Y, Li Q, Liu X, Liu G, Wang L, Li Q, Ma T, Zhang Z
evidence.evidence_sentence: 38M ma1 ma3_R: Early LD flowering; flowers earlier in LDs and has more florigen encoding transcripts than 44M.
evidence.claim: 38M ma1 ma3^R double mutant flowers very early in LD

evidence.title: Ghd7 (Ma6) Represses Sorghum Flowering in Long Days
evidence.authors: Murphy RL, Morishige DT, Brady JA, Rooney WL, Yang S, Klein PE, Mullet JE
evidence.evidence_sentence: In sorghum grown in LD, PRR37 was found to reduce the expression of SbEhd1 and to act together with Ma6 to inhibit flowering...Ghd7 confers photoperiod sensitivity and delays flowering in LDs, even in genotypes that are null for PRR37.
evidence.claim: ma1 ma6 double mutant loses both LD repressors, flowers earliest

evidence.title: Sorghum Phytochrome B Inhibits Flowering in Long Days
evidence.authors: Yang S, Weers BD, Morishige DT, Mullet JE
evidence.evidence_sentence: ma3Rma3R ma5ma5 double recessive PhyB and Ma5...Early, similar to single mutants.
evidence.claim: phyB phyC double null flowers early in LD

evidence.title: Maturity2, a novel regulator of flowering time in Sorghum bicolor
evidence.authors: Casto AL, McKinley BA, Yu KMJ, Rooney WL, Mullet JE
evidence.evidence_sentence: ma1ma2 genotypes flower earlier than ma1Ma2.
evidence.claim: ma1 ma2 double mutant flowers earlier than ma1 alone

evidence.title: Sorghum Phytochrome B Inhibits Flowering in Long Days
evidence.authors: Yang S, Weers BD, Morishige DT, Mullet JE
evidence.evidence_sentence: 100M PHYB Normal genotype Long Days vs Short Days: 126 vs 59 days; LD causes LATE flowering.
evidence.claim: LD (14h) delays flowering by ~67 days vs SD (10h) in photoperiod-sensitive 100M

evidence.title: Photoperiod response and floral transition in sorghum
evidence.authors: Cai Y, Li Q, Liu X, Liu G, Wang L, Li Q, Ma T, Zhang Z
evidence.evidence_sentence: BTx623 (photoperiod-insensitive)...flowers in a comparable time under LD and SD conditions.
evidence.claim: Photoperiod-insensitive BTx623 flowers at same time in LD vs SD

evidence.title: Coincident light and clock regulation of PRR37 controls photoperiodic flowering in sorghum
evidence.authors: Murphy RL, Klein RR, Morishige DT, Brady JA, Rooney WL, Miller FR, Dugas DV, Klein PE, Mullet JE
evidence.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
evidence.evidence_sentence: In DD, neither peak of SbPRR37 expression was observed.
evidence.claim: Continuous darkness eliminates SbPRR37 expression (no photoperiod regulation)

evidence.title: Three FLOWERING LOCUS T-like genes function as potential florigens
evidence.authors: Wolabu TW, Zhang F, Niu L, Kalve S, Bhatnagar-Mathur P, Muszynski MG, Tadege M
evidence.evidence_sentence: activate flowering in transgenic Arabidopsis plants, suggesting florigenic potential.
evidence.claim: SbFT1 OE in Arabidopsis accelerates flowering

evidence.title: Three FLOWERING LOCUS T-like genes function as potential florigens
evidence.authors: Wolabu TW, Zhang F, Niu L, Kalve S, Bhatnagar-Mathur P, Muszynski MG, Tadege M
evidence.evidence_sentence: activate flowering in transgenic Arabidopsis plants.
evidence.claim: SbFT8 OE in Arabidopsis accelerates flowering

evidence.title: Three FLOWERING LOCUS T-like genes function as potential florigens
evidence.authors: Wolabu TW, Zhang F, Niu L, Kalve S, Bhatnagar-Mathur P, Muszynski MG, Tadege M
evidence.evidence_sentence: activate flowering in transgenic Arabidopsis plants.
evidence.claim: SbFT10 OE in Arabidopsis accelerates flowering

evidence.title: Photoperiod Control of Gibberellin Levels and Flowering in Sorghum
evidence.authors: Foster KR, Morgan PW
evidence.evidence_sentence: under inductive 10-h photoperiods, the peak of GA20 and GA1 levels was shifted to an early morning peak, and flowering was hastened.
evidence.claim: Morning GA peaks correlate with accelerated flowering in sorghum

evidence.title: Coincident light and clock regulation of PRR37 controls photoperiodic flowering in sorghum
evidence.authors: Murphy RL, Klein RR, Morishige DT, Brady JA, Rooney WL, Miller FR, Dugas DV, Klein PE, Mullet JE
evidence.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
evidence.evidence_sentence: R.07007 (SbPRR37 functional + ma5 recessive) Early flowering ~68d...Altered evening SbPRR37 peak.
evidence.claim: R.07007 Ma1 Ma5-null flowers early (~68 days) in LD

evidence.title: Sorghum Phytochrome B Inhibits Flowering in Long Days
evidence.authors: Yang S, Weers BD, Morishige DT, Mullet JE
evidence.evidence_sentence: 100M (PHYB) Normal genotype...~126 days LD.
evidence.claim: Ma3 wild-type flowers late in LD (~126 days)

evidence.title: Sorghum Phytochrome B Inhibits Flowering in Long Days
evidence.authors: Yang S, Weers BD, Morishige DT, Mullet JE
evidence.evidence_sentence: SbCN15 low expression 100M (PHYB) LD or SD Delayed in LD...Associated with LATE.
evidence.claim: SbCN15/SbFT1 low expression correlates with late LD flowering

evidence.title: Genes and pathways determining flowering time variation in temperate-adapted sorghum
evidence.authors: Lemmon ZH, Stitzer MC, Jensen SE, Lovell JT, Siahkamari E, Morris GP, Washburn JD
evidence.evidence_sentence: Chr09:62,620,720 minor allele (near mir172a): Earlier flowering (p=5.93×10⁻¹⁷ Nebraska, p=7.80×10⁻⁵ Alabama).
evidence.claim: sbi-MIR172a high-expressing allele confers earlier flowering

evidence.title: Genes and pathways determining flowering time variation in temperate-adapted sorghum
evidence.authors: Lemmon ZH, Stitzer MC, Jensen SE, Lovell JT, Siahkamari E, Morris GP, Washburn JD
evidence.evidence_sentence: SbSBP19/SbSBP4 high expression: Earlier flowering (FDR = 4.37×10⁻⁹, validated Alabama p=5.38×10⁻⁵).
evidence.claim: SbSBP19 high expression confers earlier flowering

evidence.title: Genes and pathways determining flowering time variation in temperate-adapted sorghum
evidence.authors: Lemmon ZH, Stitzer MC, Jensen SE, Lovell JT, Siahkamari E, Morris GP, Washburn JD
evidence.evidence_sentence: sbi-MIR156h high expression: Represses SBP factors → delayed flowering.
evidence.claim: sbi-MIR156h high expression delays flowering (via SBP repression)

evidence.title: Genes and pathways determining flowering time variation in temperate-adapted sorghum
evidence.authors: Lemmon ZH, Stitzer MC, Jensen SE, Lovell JT, Siahkamari E, Morris GP, Washburn JD
evidence.evidence_sentence: SbFT8 minor allele (G at Chr03:69,067,236): Earlier flowering (~3-5 day difference, p=2.53×10⁻⁹ Nebraska).
evidence.claim: SbFT8 minor allele confers earlier flowering by 3-5 days

evidence.title: The Evolution of Photoperiod-Insensitive Flowering in Sorghum
evidence.authors: Cuevas HE, Zhou C, Tang H, Khadke PP, Das S, Lin YR, Ge Z, Clemente T, Upadhyaya HD, Hash CT, Paterson AH
evidence.evidence_sentence: Day-neutral allele: early, consistent flowering regardless of daylength; reduction-of-function from 3-bp CAAT-box deletion.
evidence.claim: SbFT12 day-neutral (reduced function) allele confers early flowering in temperate conditions

evidence.title: The Evolution of Photoperiod-Insensitive Flowering in Sorghum
evidence.authors: Cuevas HE, Zhou C, Tang H, Khadke PP, Das S, Lin YR, Ge Z, Clemente T, Upadhyaya HD, Hash CT, Paterson AH
evidence.evidence_sentence: two conferred statistically significant delays averaging 13.1 (P = 0.03) and 24.8 days (P = 0.09) when carrying short-day S. propinquum alleles.
evidence.claim: SbFT12 tropical functional allele delays LD flowering 13-25 days

evidence.title: Photoperiod response and floral transition in sorghum
evidence.authors: Cai Y, Li Q, Liu X, Liu G, Wang L, Li Q, Ma T, Zhang Z
evidence.evidence_sentence: 44M ma2 ma3_R: Intermediate LD flowering; flowers earlier and has more transcripts than 100M.
evidence.claim: 44M ma2 ma3^R double mutant flowers moderately early in LD

evidence.title: Photoperiod response and floral transition in sorghum
evidence.authors: Cai Y, Li Q, Liu X, Liu G, Wang L, Li Q, Ma T, Zhang Z
evidence.evidence_sentence: 100M (Ma1 Ma2 Ma3 Ma6 dominant): Very Late LD flowering but rapidly in short days.
evidence.claim: 100M photoperiod-sensitive WT flowers very late in LD

evidence.title: Photoperiod response and floral transition in sorghum
evidence.authors: Cai Y, Li Q, Liu X, Liu G, Wang L, Li Q, Ma T, Zhang Z
evidence.evidence_sentence: 100M...flowers very late in LDs but rapidly in short days (SDs).
evidence.claim: Photoperiod-sensitive WT flowers early in SD

evidence.title: Photoperiod response and floral transition in sorghum
evidence.authors: Cai Y, Li Q, Liu X, Liu G, Wang L, Li Q, Ma T, Zhang Z
evidence.evidence_sentence: Theis (sweet sorghum, sensitive) Ma1: Very Late LD flowering; Leaf number: LD ~19 vs SD ≤9.
evidence.claim: Theis sweet sorghum flowers very late in LD

evidence.title: Coincident light and clock regulation of PRR37 controls photoperiodic flowering in sorghum
evidence.authors: Murphy RL, Klein RR, Morishige DT, Brady JA, Rooney WL, Miller FR, Dugas DV, Klein PE, Mullet JE
evidence.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
evidence.evidence_sentence: SbPRR37 low evening expression (LD) associated with early flowering.
evidence.claim: Reduced SbPRR37 expression (knockdown/null) confers early LD flowering

evidence.title: Ghd7 (Ma6) Represses Sorghum Flowering in Long Days
evidence.authors: Murphy RL, Morishige DT, Brady JA, Rooney WL, Yang S, Klein PE, Mullet JE
evidence.evidence_sentence: Ghd7 suppression: Earlier flowering via loss of LD repression (breeding target).
evidence.claim: SbGhd7 RNAi/knockdown confers earlier LD flowering

evidence.title: Maturity2, a novel regulator of flowering time in Sorghum bicolor
evidence.authors: Casto AL, McKinley BA, Yu KMJ, Rooney WL, Mullet JE
evidence.evidence_sentence: In a dominant Ma4 background, a dominant allele at Ma2 delays flowering, while in a recessive ma4 background, Ma2 has a minimal impact.
evidence.claim: ma4 recessive reduces LD delay (independently and via Ma2 epistasis)

evidence.title: Mapping of QTLs underlying flowering time in sorghum
evidence.authors: Higgins RH, Thurber CS, Assaranurak I, Brown PJ
evidence.evidence_sentence: qFT6b → Likely corresponds to Ma1 (PRR37); effect size 5.3-7.1 days in kafir families.
evidence.claim: Heterozygous Ma1 alleles show intermediate LD flowering

evidence.title: Sorghum SbGhd7 is a major regulator of floral transition
evidence.authors: Tadesse T, Lugassi N, Dragonas B, Shmuel YH, Gladman N, Yuan Y, Weers B, Mullet JE, Pasternak T, Leshkowitz D, Levy AA
evidence.evidence_sentence: SbEhd1 is a key flowering activator in sorghum, directly regulating florigen genes.
evidence.claim: sbehd1 loss delays flowering by reducing florigen expression

evidence.title: Association analysis of photoperiodic flowering time genes in west and central African sorghum
evidence.authors: Bhosale SU, Stich B, Rattunde HFW, Weltzien E, Haussmann BIG, Hash CT, Melchinger AE, Parzies HK
evidence.evidence_sentence: Purifying selection has acted on genes involved in photoperiodic control (CCA1, LHY, TOC1, GI).
evidence.claim: CCA1 allelic variation shows selection on flowering-time regulation

evidence.title: Association analysis of photoperiodic flowering time genes in west and central African sorghum
evidence.authors: Bhosale SU, Stich B, Rattunde HFW, Weltzien E, Haussmann BIG, Hash CT, Melchinger AE, Parzies HK
evidence.evidence_sentence: GIGANTEA SNP888: +8 day effect on PRI (increases photoperiod sensitivity).
evidence.claim: SbGI allelic variant SNP888 increases photoperiod response index by 8 days

evidence.title: Association analysis of photoperiodic flowering time genes in west and central African sorghum
evidence.authors: Bhosale SU, Stich B, Rattunde HFW, Weltzien E, Haussmann BIG, Hash CT, Melchinger AE, Parzies HK
evidence.evidence_sentence: CRY1-b1 SNP722 (T→A transition): -4.2 day effect on PRI (reduces photoperiod sensitivity).
evidence.claim: SbCRY1 loss-of-function allele reduces photoperiod-dependent flowering delay by ~4 days

evidence.title: Photoperiod response and floral transition in sorghum
evidence.authors: Cai Y, Li Q, Liu X, Liu G, Wang L, Li Q, Ma T, Zhang Z
evidence.evidence_sentence: Sorghum is a short-day plant; night-break during SD reverses the induction to LD-like flowering delay.
evidence.claim: Night-break treatment in SD delays flowering in sensitive sorghum (mimics LD)

evidence.title: Deleterious mutations predicted in sorghum Maturity and Dwarf genes
evidence.authors: Thurber CS, Ma JM, Higgins RH, Brown PJ
evidence.evidence_sentence: The dw2 mutation is tightly linked to a maturation gene, ma1; a strong QTL of flowering time overlaps this region.
evidence.claim: dw2 linked to ma1 region; contributes to LD earliness

evidence.title: Phytochrome B and the Regulation of Circadian Ethylene Production in Sorghum
evidence.authors: Finlayson SA, Lee IJ, Morgan PW
evidence.evidence_sentence: Dim, far-red-enriched light duplicates the mutant's shade-avoidance phenotype in wild-type plants.
evidence.claim: Far-red supplementation mimics phyB loss (early flowering-like shade avoidance)

evidence.title: CONSTANS is a photoperiod regulated activator of flowering in sorghum
evidence.authors: Yang S, Murphy RL, Morishige DT, Klein PE, Rooney WL, Mullet JE
evidence.evidence_sentence: CO has greater activity in SD compared to LD causing up to 10-fold higher expression of SbCN8/12 during the night.
evidence.claim: SbCO OE under SD expected to further accelerate flowering via florigen activation

evidence.title: Sorghum SbGhd7 is a major regulator of floral transition
evidence.authors: Tadesse T, Lugassi N, Dragonas B, Shmuel YH, Gladman N, Yuan Y, Weers B, Mullet JE, Pasternak T, Leshkowitz D, Levy AA
evidence.evidence_sentence: SbFT10...major florigen.
evidence.claim: SbFT10 loss delays flowering; SbFT10 is the major florigen

evidence.title: Three FLOWERING LOCUS T-like genes function as potential florigens
evidence.authors: Wolabu TW, Zhang F, Niu L, Kalve S, Bhatnagar-Mathur P, Muszynski MG, Tadege M
evidence.evidence_sentence: Three genes, designated as SbFT1, SbFT8 and SbFT10, behave as functional Hd3a/RFT1/ZCN8 orthologs.
evidence.claim: Triple-florigen loss would abolish floral induction in sorghum

evidence.title: Genes and pathways determining flowering time variation in temperate-adapted sorghum
evidence.authors: Lemmon ZH, Stitzer MC, Jensen SE, Lovell JT, Siahkamari E, Morris GP, Washburn JD
evidence.evidence_sentence: SbMADS31 (Sobic.004G003434) encodes a SOC1 MADS-box...regulators of floral initiation.
evidence.claim: SbMADS31/SOC1 loss delays floral meristem identity acquisition

evidence.title: Genes and pathways determining flowering time variation in temperate-adapted sorghum
evidence.authors: Lemmon ZH, Stitzer MC, Jensen SE, Lovell JT, Siahkamari E, Morris GP, Washburn JD
evidence.evidence_sentence: ortholog of the maize gene rap2.7 which acts as a negative regulator of flowering time.
evidence.claim: SbAP2 loss relieves flowering repression, advances flowering

evidence.title: Genes and pathways determining flowering time variation in temperate-adapted sorghum
evidence.authors: Lemmon ZH, Stitzer MC, Jensen SE, Lovell JT, Siahkamari E, Morris GP, Washburn JD
evidence.evidence_sentence: SbFT2 (Sobic.003G017200) expression in mature leaf tissue was significantly associated with observed flowering time.
evidence.claim: SbFT2 high expression confers earlier flowering

evidence.title: Coincident light and clock regulation of PRR37 controls photoperiodic flowering in sorghum
evidence.authors: Murphy RL, Klein RR, Morishige DT, Brady JA, Rooney WL, Miller FR, Dugas DV, Klein PE, Mullet JE
evidence.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
evidence.evidence_sentence: ATx623 Sbprr37-3 (dual mutations)...Early flowering...Insensitive under both LD and SD.
evidence.claim: BTx623/ATx623 ma1 null flowers at similar time in LD vs SD

evidence.title: Coincident light and clock regulation of PRR37 controls photoperiodic flowering in sorghum
evidence.authors: Murphy RL, Klein RR, Morishige DT, Brady JA, Rooney WL, Miller FR, Dugas DV, Klein PE, Mullet JE
evidence.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
evidence.evidence_sentence: 100M (SbPRR37 functional)...Late flowering (~120d) LD.
evidence.claim: Dominant SbPRR37 (Ma1) delays LD flowering to ~120 days

evidence.title: Ghd7 (Ma6) Represses Sorghum Flowering in Long Days
evidence.authors: Murphy RL, Morishige DT, Brady JA, Rooney WL, Yang S, Klein PE, Mullet JE
evidence.evidence_sentence: Ghd7 confers photoperiod sensitivity and delays flowering in LDs, even in genotypes that are null for PRR37.
evidence.claim: Dominant SbGhd7 (Ma6) delays flowering in LD even in prr37-null

evidence.title: Sorghum bicolor INDETERMINATE1 is a conserved primary regulator of flowering
evidence.authors: Gladman N, Goodwin S, Chougule K, Richard McCombie W, Ware D
evidence.evidence_sentence: sbid1-1/sbid1-2 compound: Phenotype matches sbid1-2, confirming loss-of-function.
evidence.claim: Complementation with WT SbID1 restores flowering timing

evidence.title: Molecular Evolution of the Sorghum Maturity Gene Ma3
evidence.authors: Wang Y, Tan L, Fu Y, Zhu Z, Liu F, Sun C, Cai H
evidence.evidence_sentence: Ma3 (wild-type): Normal photoperiod sensitivity; selected against loss-of-function.
evidence.claim: Ma3 dominant allele confers LD flowering delay (sensitivity)

evidence.title: Association analysis of photoperiodic flowering time genes in west and central African sorghum
evidence.authors: Bhosale SU, Stich B, Rattunde HFW, Weltzien E, Haussmann BIG, Hash CT, Melchinger AE, Parzies HK

evidence.claim: SbPhyA loss shows minor/unclear effect on flowering (secondary photoreceptor)

evidence.title: Sorghum SbGhd7 is a major regulator of floral transition

evidence.evidence_sentence: repressing transcription of...SbFKF1...direct target of SbGhd7.

evidence.title: Sorghum SbGhd7 is a major regulator of floral transition
evidence.authors: Tadesse T, Lugassi N, Dragonas B, Shmuel YH, Gladman N, Yuan Y, Weers B, Mullet JE, Pasternak T, Leshkowitz D, Levy AA
evidence.evidence_sentence: repressing transcription of...SbELF3.
evidence.claim: SbELF3 (repressed by Ghd7) — its loss in cereals typically advances flowering

evidence.title: Sorghum Phytochrome B Inhibits Flowering in Long Days
evidence.authors: Yang S, Weers BD, Morishige DT, Mullet JE
evidence.evidence_sentence: PhyB absent + Ma6 ma3Rma3R Ma6_ 42-75 days (range) EARLY (variable).
evidence.claim: ma3^R in various Ma1/Ma6 backgrounds flowers early (42-75 days LD)

evidence.title: A sorghum gigantea mutant attenuates florigen gene expression and delays flowering time
evidence.authors: Abdul-Awal SM, Chen J, Xin Z, Harmon FG
evidence.evidence_sentence: average DTB for sbgi-ems1 plants was greater than WT sibling plants by 28 (Berkeley) and 22 (Davis) days.
evidence.claim: sbgi-ems1 delays field flowering by 22-28 days

evidence.title: A sorghum gigantea mutant attenuates florigen gene expression and delays flowering time
evidence.authors: Abdul-Awal SM, Chen J, Xin Z, Harmon FG
evidence.evidence_sentence: sbgi-ems1 mutant plants made an average of 2.4 more nodes than WT.
evidence.claim: sbgi extends vegetative phase (+2.4 nodes before flowering)

evidence.title: CONSTANS is a photoperiod regulated activator of flowering in sorghum
evidence.authors: Yang S, Murphy RL, Morishige DT, Klein PE, Rooney WL, Mullet JE
evidence.evidence_sentence: Ability of SbPRR37 to inhibit flowering is dependent on SbCO — PRR37 effects vanish in Sbco-3 backgrounds.
evidence.claim: sbco-3 is epistatic to SbPRR37; combined genotype flowers early

evidence.title: Photoperiod Control of Gibberellin Levels and Flowering in Sorghum
evidence.authors: Foster KR, Morgan PW
evidence.evidence_sentence: GA levels correlate with flowering; GA biosynthesis inhibitors that reduce GA1/GA20 would delay flowering.
evidence.claim: Paclobutrazol (GA biosynthesis inhibitor) expected to delay sorghum flowering

evidence.title: Genes and pathways determining flowering time variation in temperate-adapted sorghum
evidence.authors: Lemmon ZH, Stitzer MC, Jensen SE, Lovell JT, Siahkamari E, Morris GP, Washburn JD
evidence.evidence_sentence: SbMADS7 (Sobic.002G010100)...regulators of floral initiation.
evidence.claim: SbAP1 (MADS7) loss delays floral meristem identity

evidence.title: Molecular Evolution of the Sorghum Maturity Gene Ma3
evidence.authors: Wang Y, Tan L, Fu Y, Zhu Z, Liu F, Sun C, Cai H
evidence.evidence_sentence: ma3R: Nearly complete photoperiod insensitivity caused by a single base-pair deletion in exon 3, resulting in early flowering regardless of day length.
evidence.claim: ma3^R deletion mutant flowers early regardless of photoperiod

evidence.title: Ghd7 (Ma6) Represses Sorghum Flowering in Long Days
evidence.authors: Murphy RL, Morishige DT, Brady JA, Rooney WL, Yang S, Klein PE, Mullet JE
evidence.evidence_sentence: In sorghum grown in LD, PRR37...acts together with Ma6 to inhibit flowering...SD already permissive for flowering.
evidence.claim: ma6 SD has minimal effect (SD already permissive)

evidence.title: Maturity2, a novel regulator of flowering time in Sorghum bicolor
evidence.authors: Casto AL, McKinley BA, Yu KMJ, Rooney WL, Mullet JE
evidence.evidence_sentence: Ma2 acts downstream of circadian clock and phytochromes, increasing SbPRR37 and SbCO expression in long days.
evidence.claim: ma2 has minimal SD effect (Ma2 LD-specific action)

evidence.title: The sorghum photoperiod sensitivity gene, Ma3, encodes a phytochrome B
evidence.authors: Childs KL, Miller FR, Cordonnier-Pratt MM, Pratt LH, Morgan PW, Mullet JE
evidence.evidence_sentence: ma3R (null)...Early/insensitive to photoperiod.
evidence.claim: ma3^R phyB-null flowers early under LD, insensitive to photoperiod

evidence.title: OsELF3-1, an ortholog of Arabidopsis early flowering 3, regulates rice circadian rhythm and photoperiodic flowering
evidence.authors: Zhao J, Huang X, Ouyang X, Chen W, Du A, Zhu L, Wang S, Deng XW, Li S
evidence.evidence_sentence: oself3-1 mutant flowers earlier than wild-type...OsELF3-1 suppresses Ghd7 to indirectly accelerate flowering under LD.
evidence.claim: Rice oself3 flowers early under LD; inferred behaviour for sorghum SbELF3 KO

evidence.title: Photoperiod response and floral transition in sorghum
evidence.authors: Cai Y, Li Q, Liu X, Liu G, Wang L, Li Q, Ma T, Zhang Z
evidence.evidence_sentence: SbFT1 directly interacts with SbFD1...FAC assembly required for AP1/SOC1 activation.
evidence.claim: SbFD1 loss would abolish FAC function and delay flowering (canonical FAC module inference)

evidence.title: Photoperiod response and floral transition in sorghum
evidence.authors: Cai Y, Li Q, Liu X, Liu G, Wang L, Li Q, Ma T, Zhang Z
evidence.evidence_sentence: SbFT1/8/10 all directly interact with Sb14-3-3 proteins...florigen activation complex.
evidence.claim: Sb14-3-3 knockdown would disrupt FAC scaffolding and delay floral transition

evidence.title: Ghd7 (Ma6) Represses Sorghum Flowering in Long Days
evidence.authors: Murphy RL, Morishige DT, Brady JA, Rooney WL, Yang S, Klein PE, Mullet JE
evidence.evidence_sentence: ma5 and ma6 double recessive produces thermosensitive early flowering in classical Quinby genetics; ma6 confers LD earliness.
evidence.claim: ma5 ma6 double recessive flowers earlier than either single mutant under LD (thermosensitive background)
